constituents: Manisuris hirsuta contains a range of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant's active constituents have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The flavonoids present in Manisuris hirsuta may also contribute to its potential therapeutic effects on cardiovascular health and cognitive function. Furthermore, the plant's phenolic acids have been found to have antitumor properties, suggesting its potential use in cancer treatment. However, more research is needed to fully understand the mechanisms of action of Manisuris hirsuta's active constituents.
